user_query:
I'm helping my brother track how his art therapy is going, and I want to see week-by-week trends in his mood, anxiety, and common themes. Using the local inputs below, please build and run a small command-line script (any language you prefer) that reads the logs and writes structured results. Please do not fetch anything from the internet.

Inputs (already in the workspace):
- input/session_logs.jsonl
- input/art_activities.csv
- input/stopwords.txt

What to produce:
1) output/weekly_metrics.json
   - JSON array sorted by week_start ascending (Monday as the week start). Each object must include:
     - week_start: ISO date string for the Monday of that week (e.g., 2023-05-01)
     - sessions: integer count of sessions that week
     - avg_mood: numeric average of "mood" for that week
     - avg_anxiety: numeric average of "anxiety" for that week
     - avg_duration_minutes: numeric average of "duration_minutes" for that week
     - dominant_activity_category: category string derived by mapping the session "activity" via input/art_activities.csv; choose the category with the highest session count in that week. If there is a tie, break ties by alphabetical order of the category name.
     - top_tags: list of up to 3 tags with highest frequency that week (from the "tags" arrays in the logs). Break ties alphabetically.
     - top_keywords: list of up to 5 keywords from the free-text "notes" that week after lowercasing, removing punctuation and non-letters, splitting on whitespace, and removing any words present in input/stopwords.txt; keep tokens of length >= 3 only. Sort by frequency desc, then alphabetically.

2) output/keyword_trends.csv
   - CSV with header: week_start,keyword,count
   - Include one row per (week_start, keyword) where the keyword (from notes after the same normalization and stopword removal as above) appears at least 2 times within that week. Sort by week_start ascending, then count descending, then keyword ascending.

3) output/report.md
   - A readable summary that includes:
     - A line reporting the least-squares slope across weeks for avg_mood and for avg_anxiety (computed from the series in output/weekly_metrics.json against week indices starting at 0). Show each slope rounded to two decimals.
     - A classification line for each: "Mood trend: Improving|Declining|Stable" where Improving if slope >= 0.10, Declining if slope <= -0.10, else Stable; and for anxiety, interpret improvement as a negative slope (i.e., "Anxiety trend: Improving" if slope <= -0.10, Declining if slope >= 0.10, else Stable).
     - A bullet list where each item is "- WEEK_START: nature_count=N" giving the count N of the tag "nature" in that week (0 if absent), derived from the tags in the logs.

Operational requirements:
- Read all three input files and use them as described (the activities mapping must be used to determine dominant_activity_category; the stopwords must be used for keyword extraction from notes).
- Implement and run your script locally to generate the artifacts above. Place the exact command you ran (relative to repo root) into output/run_command.txt so I can re-run it.
- Do not hardcode weekly values; compute them from the provided inputs.

Input paths to use:
- input/session_logs.jsonl
- input/art_activities.csv
- input/stopwords.txt

Expected output paths:
- output/weekly_metrics.json
- output/keyword_trends.csv
- output/report.md
- output/run_command.txt

Thanks—this will help me encourage the kinds of art activities that seem to calm him over time. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
